client_context_template: Client is a mid-size U.S. tech company buying a production {service_name} service that will process customer records. Procurement requires visibility into hosting location/cross-border transfer basis and certified deletion at exit; absence of those details should be escalated.
instruction_template: Review the vendor MSA against the playbook. Flag deviations with exact quotes, propose fallback text where required, and escalate missing information.
preamble_template: This Master Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by and between {customer_name} ("{customer_role}") and {vendor_name} ("{vendor_role}") as of the effective date of the first Order Form.

sections.heading: Services and Ordering
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will provide hosted {service_name}, normalization, mapping, and workflow services described in one or more signed order forms or statements of work (each, an "Order Form"). Each Order Form is governed by this Agreement. If an Order Form conflicts with this Agreement, the Order Form controls only for that Order Form and only to the extent it expressly identifies the conflicting provision.

sections.heading: Defined Terms
paragraphs: "Customer Data" means information, files, records, identifiers, and other content submitted to the service by or for {customer_role} or generated from {customer_role}'s use of the service. "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, including project-specific tools, frameworks, and know-how developed in the course of performance. "Vendor Methods" means {vendor_role}'s templates, scripts, accelerators, schemas, libraries, routines, methods, concepts, ideas, skills, and general learning existing before the engagement or developed independently outside the services. "Security Incident" means any suspected or actual unauthorized access to, acquisition of, use of, loss of, or disclosure of Customer Data.

sections.heading: Customer Responsibilities
paragraphs: {customer_role} will provide reasonable access to personnel, systems, sample data, and business rules needed for {vendor_role} to perform the services. {customer_role} is responsible for the accuracy, legality, and completeness of instructions and materials it provides, and for obtaining consents required for {customer_role}'s own collection of Customer Data before submission to the service.

sections.heading: Service Standards
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will perform the services in a professional and workmanlike manner using personnel with appropriate skill and experience. {vendor_role} will materially conform to the specifications in the applicable Order Form and will correct nonconforming services at no additional charge if {customer_role} gives written notice within thirty days after delivery.

sections.heading: Data Handling
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will process Customer Data only to provide and support the services, to comply with {customer_role}'s documented instructions, and as otherwise expressly authorized in an Order Form. {vendor_role} will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect Customer Data against accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure, or access. {vendor_role} will comply with laws applicable to {vendor_role}'s provision of the services.

sections.heading: Notice Regarding Protected Records
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will notify {customer_role} without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours after discovering a Security Incident. The alert will summarize known facts, affected systems, remedial steps taken or planned, and a contact for follow-up. {vendor_role} will reasonably cooperate with {customer_role}'s investigation and customer communications.

sections.heading: Service Partners
paragraphs: {vendor_role} may use affiliates, hosting providers, implementation contractors, and other service partners to perform portions of the services, provided {vendor_role} remains responsible for their work and binds each service partner to written obligations at least as protective as {vendor_role}'s obligations for Customer Data, confidentiality, security, and service performance.

sections.heading: Ownership and Licenses
paragraphs: Upon payment of applicable fees, {vendor_role} assigns to {customer_role} all right, title, and interest in the Deliverables prepared specifically for {customer_role} under an Order Form. {vendor_role} retains ownership of Vendor Methods. To the extent Vendor Methods are embedded in a Deliverable, {vendor_role} grants {customer_role} a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, display, and create internal modifications of those Vendor Methods as part of {customer_role}'s use of the Deliverables.

sections.heading: Fees, Invoices, and Taxes
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will invoice {customer_role} as stated in the applicable Order Form. Unless an Order Form states otherwise, undisputed invoices are due forty-five days after {customer_role} receives the invoice. {customer_role} may withhold disputed amounts in good faith if it gives {vendor_role} reasonable detail regarding the dispute and pays undisputed amounts when due. {vendor_role} may charge interest on undisputed overdue amounts at one percent per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower.

sections.heading: Term and Extension
paragraphs: This Agreement begins on the effective date of the first Order Form and continues while any Order Form remains in effect. Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and will not automatically extend unless {customer_role} signs a renewal order or gives written approval for a renewal period of one year or less.

sections.heading: Customer Exit Right
paragraphs: {customer_role} may terminate any Order Form for convenience on thirty days' written notice. {customer_role} will pay fees for services performed before the effective termination date and approved reimbursable expenses incurred before that date.

sections.heading: Transition Assistance
paragraphs: Upon expiration or termination, {vendor_role} will provide reasonable transition assistance at its then-current professional services rates for up to thirty days if requested by {customer_role}. If {customer_role} ends an Order Form without {vendor_role} default, {customer_role} will pay only accrued, undisputed fees and approved reimbursable expenses incurred before the effective date.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
paragraphs: Each party will protect the other party's non-public business, technical, financial, and product information using at least reasonable care and will use that information only to perform or receive services under this Agreement. These obligations do not apply to information that the receiving party can document was already known without restriction, became public without breach, was independently developed without use of the information, or was lawfully received from a third party without duty.

sections.heading: Third-Party Claims
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will defend and indemnify {customer_role} against third-party claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights or that {vendor_role}'s performance violates applicable law. {customer_role} will defend and indemnify {vendor_role} against third-party claims arising from Customer materials, {customer_role}'s unlawful use of the services, or use of the services contrary to this Agreement. The indemnified party must promptly notify the indemnifying party, provide reasonable cooperation, and allow control of the defense subject to the indemnified party's right to participate at its own expense.

sections.heading: Warranties and Disclaimers
paragraphs: Except for express warranties and commitments stated in this Agreement or an Order Form, {vendor_role} disclaims implied warranties, including implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. This disclaimer does not limit {vendor_role}'s confidentiality duties, data handling duties, indemnities, service-level commitments, or remedies expressly stated in this Agreement.

sections.heading: Liability
paragraphs: Except for breaches of confidentiality, data handling obligations, indemnification obligations, intellectual property infringement, fraud, willful misconduct, and gross negligence, each party's aggregate liability arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not exceed the fees paid or payable under the affected Order Form during the twelve months before the event giving rise to the claim. The foregoing limitation does not limit {customer_role}'s obligation to pay undisputed fees.

sections.heading: Insurance and Audit
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will maintain commercially reasonable insurance at its own expense, including technology errors and omissions/cyber liability coverage of at least $5,000,000 per claim and commercial general liability coverage of at least $2,000,000 per occurrence. Upon reasonable notice, {customer_role} or its independent auditor may review {vendor_role}'s records, systems, and controls relevant to the services, security, privacy, and compliance no more than once per year, and more frequently after a material incident or reasonable cause.

sections.heading: General Terms
paragraphs: Neither party may assign this Agreement without the other party's prior written consent, except to an affiliate or successor in connection with a merger, reorganization, or sale of substantially all assets if the assignee assumes the assigning party's obligations. No amendment, modification, or waiver is effective unless in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of {law_state}, without regard to conflict-of-law rules. The parties are independent contractors. Notices must be in writing and delivered by courier, certified mail, or email with confirmation of receipt. Despite any contrary sentence, the limitation in Section 16 does not apply to claims under Section 13 or Section 14.

distractors.span: Unless an Order Form states otherwise, undisputed invoices are due forty-five days after {customer_role} receives the invoice.
distractors.why_benign: The payment canary says net-45 terms after receipt of an undisputed invoice are acceptable.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of {law_state}, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
distractors.why_benign: The governing-law canary says {law_state} law is acceptable.

distractors.span: {vendor_role} may charge interest on undisputed overdue amounts at one percent per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower.
distractors.why_benign: A capped late-payment charge on undisputed overdue amounts is outside this playbook.

missing_info.topic: Processing geography and cross-border transfer basis
